title: sabina jaliscana
intro: Sabina jaliscana, also known as the Mexican juniper or sabina, is a small evergreen shrub native to Mexico, belonging to the cypress family (Cupressaceae). The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sabina jaliscana include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be ingested or applied topically to the skin.
